sentences.text: Lookit, Justin's got that Arizona salmon polo on.
I mean… That's such an Arizona look right there.
Been on the spare bed behind me for about a month, haven't washed it, so… Whatever call, I throw it on.
Alternate between that and the green one, so…
That's funny.
That's funny.
Hunter's not too dissimilar.
We've seen a lot of this striped shirt that Hunter wears here.
Yeah, this is the closing shirt.
This is what got SBM done in two years.
Oh, this is the two-year sales cycle shirt, it's running.
So I thought it was very fitting for today's goal.
That is fitting, right?
Lightning quick with Mark Taylor, I feel like, so…
Well, guys, I know we've been here before, but this is, exciting to be back on with Mark Taylor.
Justin's like, yeah, we've already demoed this group before, but.
We're gonna get them.
I mean, sheer, just… Relentless.
Relentlessness.
Relentlessness will get this one done, I hope.
Someday.
Someday.
What do we know?
Co-Star?
What do you remember from that?
Justin, I think you did that demo solo with Adam's team forever ago.
Yeah, that was about a year ago.
What I got here?
I think it was CoStar.
And then, I think they were just kind of getting into Red IQ.
I think.
But Raddix, Kosar and Raddix.
Yeah.
Their whole thing, too, is, like.
what's tough with these guys is they, like, we run the Arizona market, like.
But it…
They, they know everything that's going on, they're, they're, they run, they kind of set the bar and everything, so…
That was part of it.
But Adam's BizDev?
Generally, but he has analysts on his team, is that right?
That's right, yeah.
So these guys… I looked up one of the users, but, they had… these guys had trial access before, so research and… Market surveys.
Okay.
So what are you… how do we want to approach things today?
I think a quick refresh in the market survey, be like, hey, I know you guys have seen this before, but kind of a new layout.
Maybe talk about the new floor plan page, which I don't think is new.
I mean, even, too, I was thinking about this, and…
Forecasting, I don't have access to forecasting.
Okay.
I think that'll be cool, though.
Especially if… well, right now, it's just at the MSA level.
Yeah.
But I think that other information, like the ownership group information, I don't know if that's necessarily big for them.
I don't know how much they're actually reaching out for third-party managing.
Well, atoms… in theory, you would be, right?
Thank you.
You'd think it'd be… the use case would be use, you know, use the occupancy filter, use the…
Yeah.
management company or ownership company, filter, check boxes, selection, if you want, you know.
Filtered.
filter mark… sorry, filter a neighborhood in… in… Scottsdale or Phoenix.
by occupancy, find your target list, I think?
Yeah, right.

I think… I think if we do little discoveries to just see, like, how the heck they're using, and first off, see what they're still using, and see, like, how they're using it, and then I can go from there.
I mean, I can…
But…
Hunter, you want to kick us off?
I'll play color.
Justin, you jump in and wrap it up.
I think we can all contribute a little bit here, and…

Yeah, I think it's almost like a little bit of a feature dump, too, like, listen, like, we have everything here now, like, be like, you guys don't need to go with Radix, CoStar, all that shit, like, we have every… I think it's worth showing them the, the rent rule, I think it's… I mean, that's the stuff I want to show them.
I think it's really unique, so…
Okay.
But we shouldn't be showing things like forecasting probably quite… quite yet in this environment anyways, but yeah, we can hint at it if it seems, relevant, and…
Because I don't think they have, besides CoStar, they don't have another data provider anymore.
Apparently.
Allegedly.
Well, they're… they're… whenever we met with them, wasn't their RADX agreement ending, like, this summer?
Like, I think it's better to check on that, so…
He's at home.
Yeah.
So, we need to confirm that.
Allie talked to… is it Mike?
And he… he said they were just using CoStar, at the time.
I think that was, like, 2 months ago, so… but let's… let's confirm it, for sure.
I think Adam does more than just…

business development, so we should… we should just make sure we understand roles and who's on, what they do, what tools they use, what problems they're trying to solve, just the basics, I think, would be helpful.
Yup.
They only… and this isn't true.
This isn't true.
I was gonna say the only meeting I've flown to for a single meeting, the Adam Lunch, but that's not true.
That's probably the lowest value lunch I've ever… Take a flight to join.
It was a good conversation, too, is what sucks.
I mean, it went nowhere.
Crazy.
And this is the story of these guys.
Yeah, it was cra- I mean, I don't think any of us thought, coming out of that lunch, we wouldn't have been in their office the next week, based on how it went and what he asked for.
That's what they do, though.
I mean, for, like, what, 4 years now, Dustin's been kind of, like, jerking us off, like, he tells us, like, he tells everyone how great we are, and then… it's just what happens.

I saw… I saw him, I forgot to tell y'all this, I saw him on, like, a Saturday, at Fashion Square, like, outside of Dick's Forting Goods with his kids, and I just joked… I didn't say anything, but, like, jokingly, I thought, like, imagine going up to him and being like, what are you gonna buy from us?
That's funny.
We'll get them, we'll get them.
Okay, here he is, he joined.
I'll let him in.
Be a conference room.
Good afternoon, Adam, how are you?
Yeah, get us on to… oop!
Can you hear us?
Loud and clear, can you hear me?
Yep, doing great.
I'm just getting you onto the bigger screen here, because I didn't change my, screen orientation, of course.
Hmm.
There we go.
Adam, we've.
we are.
table a few times.
Absolutely, you know that table.
Oh, yeah.
Yep.
This won't be as good as face-to-face lunch like last time, but this is the next best thing.
Rich.
There is, and I greatly thank you again, guys.
That was a good time with you.
Sorry it took so long for us to reconnect once again.
But, glad to see that you guys have continued growing.
Hey, I wanted to, quickly introduce… I'm not sure if you've ever met Two of my very highly intelligent, hardworking team members.
I say that because I'm gonna ask them to do a lot more work after we're done with this meeting, so I'm buttering them up right now.
They have no idea what's coming.
And this is all they're gonna think about now through this demo.
I'm only teasing, I have no favors to ask of.
Please let me introduce Henry Smith Hasty.
One of our portfolio analysts, and then Ryan Abu Baker.
Helps out with our managing directors, but also an analyst as well, so… A lot of firepower in here, so learning about our government's IQ, key for us.
The world has significantly changed.
In regards to data collection, And, we're getting a little frustrated.
I can only imagine what you guys have had to go through as well, so… The floor is yours, gentlemen.
And Adam, real quick, I know last year we had a trial set up.
Were these two involved with it at all?
Have you guys seen the product before, or was there a few others?
I can't remember specifically who was involved in it.
Yeah, I do, I do remember your face, so I think I was in the demo app.
Yeah.
So, about a year ago.
Yeah, yeah, I was looking at it almost exactly a year ago, I think last August, so I wasn't sure… I thought a few of you might have seen it before, but I wasn't positive.
Well, I think.
Appreciate that.
Thanks, Justin.
It's a good thing we're talking today, Adam.
You're seemingly in a good mood before the Bears-Vikings on Monday, night coming up here, so…
I have nothing but confidence.
It's our CEO, John.
who, of course, I think you might know, he's, of course, from Minnesota.
I'm going over to his house Monday night, and I know he's gonna have that stupid horn playing constantly.
He has… did I share this with you?
He has a little Bluetooth speaker that he purposely, just when this happens, you know, these games, the NFC North Division games.

he has the horn going off at all… I mean, just sporadically, and he does it at the most inopportune times in regards to my viewpoint, being such a Bears fan, so… I don't know, I'm gonna have to swap that out, have him come to my house, and just play the, the Bears fight song over and over again.
Well, I wish you luck, I'll be, don't hold it against me, I'll be wearing my cheese head this weekend, cheering for my Packers, so, but we'll see how the season goes.
pickup, Corey, big pickup!
Micah Parsons.
Yeah.
Huge.
It's making us wear fans very nervous.
Right?
Feels a little bit like, Khalil Mack back in the day, but, yeah, we're.
often.
We're excited, so…
Good for you.
I can't wait.
Hopefully this mysterious back injury isn't, isn't, actually… Real, though, so…
Every… you know what?
You never know until they show up.
That's right, that's right.
Well, thanks for putting the group together.
We're excited to kind of… it sounds like give a refresher, I think, regarding our platform, but maybe… Maybe, kind of a new look as well, so….
Yeah, I would love… yeah, we'd love to see, you know, hear… So, if this helps, I just… I think we know each other enough.
Transparency, very, very important to all of us, not just personally, but in, you know, in the professional realm.
We moved away from Radix at the end of our contract, and during that time, we, we partnered up with CoStar.
We've never been… huge fans of CoStar, because we are ILS independent, and, you know, if you're not part of Apartments.
com.
the data, the third-party data collection piece, you know, sometimes gets a little wonky.
So, we came to the agreement, we provided a direct feed.
Into our, property management software.

And… it's incrementally getting a little bit better in certain aspects, and in other aspects, it's getting worse, so… For us, we see the value of CoStar with a bunch of their data, CNBC, CMBS loans, you know, demographics, just the whole traffic, everything, and it's not just multifamily.

you know, I could look at jobs across any type of industry, so there's a lot of benefits within the platform, but where we're truly focused on is the market surveys and the accuracy behind you know, comm sets, building out specific comm sets.
We live and die, as you guys know, here at Mark Taylor.
by our market surveys, weekly market surveys.
It's how it aids in our pricing.
As far as raising rents, lowering rents, increasing concessions, lowering concessions, where's our exposure, where are we seeing exposure on certain floor plans, within the concept as well?
It is a very big piece.
So, for us, the accuracy, the ease.
Being to… being able to create even custom market surveys for deals that may or may not happen.
That's important to us, and right now.
we can't do that within CoStar.
So we've had to make, you know, some… Exceptions.
In the world… in the third-party data world.
And so, if I'm hearing you, Adam, like, still running weekly, like, manual market surveys in addition to using the CoStar data?

Well, we run them… we, our team here, we run them, we run them and then upload them into our share drive, you know, our internal share drive, where the, MCOs, managers of community operations, can go in and then review them every single Monday.
So, for us here, we have to… it's like how many clicks of the button that we have to go through to… Too many.
Too many clicks.
It is very detailed, it's very cumbersome, and we're doing that for 140…
properties every single Monday.
We're doing that internally.
Wow.
Okay, got it.
That's the manual component.
So, pulling together 140 market surveys from COSTAR data.
Sounds like there's some issues with accuracy, certainly a clunky workflow or user experience, it sounds like.
Yes, fair to say.
Okay.
Yeah, I think, too, and we'll give you the refresher on the application here, but, I mean, if you're just taking the data and plugging it into your own workforce, I mean, we have, like.
simple data dumps we could do on a weekly or even daily basis.
So, I mean, we could even just bypass that entire manual aspect of it, but, I mean, our coverage is, one, gonna be substantially better than CoStar.
We actually had a, one of our enterprise customers that they're tracking something like 600 properties across the country, so for their properties, plus their 5, 7, 10 comps, we had 99% coverage.
CoStar had about 83%.
So, to your point, they're relying on, kind of, their network.
With us, just a reminder, I mean, we're completely system agnostic, we can track anybody with an online presence.
And then the big part of this is going to be the quality of the data.
So we're updating our data every single day.
You know, those guys, I think, are every 4 to 6 weeks.
They have, you know, call centers set up, they make phone calls into subsets of markets.

So… you know, even kind of talking with our current customers that still use CoStar for bigger picture stuff, that data is substantially lagging, especially in those markets where there are concessions and, you know, you're trying to figure out net effective rents, so data quality should be substantially better within our platform, as well as that coverage aspect that I was talking about.
Great.
Great.
As far as pricing goes, are you guys using a pricing tool, or are you pricing in-house?
We do it all in-house, we don't like pricing tools.
Okay.
You know, just a, you know, friendly reminder, with us just being here, Phoenix MSA, Las Vegas Metro.
You know, we're not… we're not national, we're regional, so pricing, this is our… we know both.
sub-market.
We know both markets, I should say, sub-markets within the, you know, overall market.
We know, like the back of our hand, I put our internal data up against anybody, and put all of my network up against that on that bed, too.
It's the… It's the collective.
It's not… so, here's, you know, what we loved, and this is no… no dig, I'm just… if it's okay with you guys, I just want to speak openly.
We loved… we loved Radix.
We loved Radix because it was direct… it was, you know, integration.
Right into the PMS.

It was a simple platform, it made sense, it was accurate, it was speedy, it was… you know, again, all the factors that were easy for our on-site teams, as well as our internal team, because we're always working with clients two years… down the road, right?
So, for us to predict what's going to happen in 2 years, that's not… it's not a reasonable thing to ask, as far as a client to ask.
Ask that of us.

But, to kind of forecast somewhat into the future, but knowing and feeling confident of what I currently have in front of me is 100% accurate to current market conditions, that was a big piece for us.
That was a big confidence booster for us, along with being able to be creative.
building custom reports, et cetera, et cetera.
Well, when the whole political landscape changed.
And I'll say this over and over again, no fault to Apartments IQ, no fault to Radix.
I think it was a bunch of bullshit, to be quite honest.
What the difference is between public and private information.
Everything is on the frickin' websites in the first place.
It's all public, right?
All you had to do was actually do some simple math.
And you can figure out what occupancy is, what… right?
It's just… it just… it's… it's crazy.
Anyways, that's the reason why that we had to pull away from Radix.
It wasn't because it was a bad relationship.
It's the political landscape, the industry change, and we are being infiltrated by outside sources that really have nothing to do with multifamily, right?
Yeah, and I think we shared the… whenever we had lunch, that we've pulled over about 2 million of their paid customer, you know, units to our platform.
And, I mean, it's temporary.
Beautiful.
exactly what we were talking about, the public versus private debt, and I think what's… kind of worked out in our favor, but that entire private database that RealPage had.
We essentially have the public version of it.
So, all of those data points, we've been tracking unit-level data every day for the last 5 years, so it's not like we just have pricing on the cheapest one-bedroom in each property.
We have every single floor plan, we have leasing velocity, we show application velocity, we have those occupancy lease exposure percentage trends.
So, we have… All of the data points that, you know, essentially they got in trouble for, just because we have the public version of it.
Yep.

And talking about, too, like, you know, talking about what's coming in the future, what's nice here is… I can't… I can't remember if we told you this or not, but we have, new supply in the application now, so you can see different stages of development in every market.

We're going to be releasing forecasting here in a matter of, weeks, so we actually brought over one of CoStar's One of their top guys, and he's been working on forecasting, so that'll be out for the top 50 MSAs here shortly.

So, I'll let you know, what's nice here is, you know, we're building, essentially, like, the one-stop shop for everything multifamily, so not just market surveys, but literally every other data point possible.
Do you guys feel confident enough, confident enough, and, Telling your forecasted number, or what's under construction in front of these two guys?
Because that's all they do.
I think so.
You want to put your data against ours?
What do you think, Henry?
See?
I think we're worried about.
We'll set you guys up with a trial again, we'll let you go to town with it.
See what you can, you know, see what you can, pressure test.
You know what, Jess, I would love… so here's… here's a big thing for us, too, is, I have to be cognizant of costs at all times, right?
It's still a very unstable type of environment that we're living in.
We know that we haven't hit rock bottom just yet, operationally.
Part of what we do, as I've shared with you previously, especially with Ryan and Henry here.
You know, everything that we're collecting across the board, not just within multifamily, even… even when it comes to macroeconomics, cash freight, you know, the supply chain.
Issues.
This all… this all rolls up into our view of how the current state of the market is, and what's going to happen in the future, as best as we can, you know, barring any kind of black swan events.
So we know that early next year is gonna really be the hardest hit when it comes operationally, just because of the amount of excess inventory.
Not… not absorption, but excess inventory.
Units that have never been leased, brand new units, let me specify, brand new units that have been delivered to the market that have never been rented.
That's what causes pain.
That's what causes high concessions, and of course, decreasing or flattening of market rents.
So I just want to be cognizant not to pass this B over to my clients.
And then have it come down the line within the next 6 to 9 months, hey, we gotta cut costs, because that's what every owner is already talking about.
Especially now, during budget season.
I'm sure you hear the same… same stuff, so… For me, I still see a lot of value in CoStar.
I mean, we are a consulting team as well.

So having the access to nationwide information, like I said before, jobs, demographics, etc, all across the line, all the different industry, is very, very important to us, although it is a very, very expensive monthly fee that I'm currently paying with CoStar.
I would love to explore, is there a way, when it comes to pricing for you and I with Apartments IQ, I should say, Apartments IQ and Mark Taylor.
How can we be creative?
If we can be creative, At all.
You know, we can be.
Yeah, I don't know, Adam, I think we've been in communication for quite some time, so, like, we don't let budgets get in the way.
We're pretty keen.
to partner with Mark Taylor, so I'm certain we can find something that works.

There are levers we can kind of work with, too, around billing start dates and get creative there to kind of delay some of this into the future, so… I think… For today, if you're comfortable, let's show you what we have and what's new.
Let's make sure this fits what you're looking for, and then we'll just… we'll work through it.
So you'll tell us, here's… you know, here's where I need to be, here's who needs to have access, and we'll do everything we can to make it work.
So…
I'll share with you guys, too, because it's worth, and you guys deserve it, a number of our ownership clients Really tout how much they love your platform.
So, you've got a lot of supporters out there, especially on the market survey side.
So kudos to you guys.
Henry, Ryan, before they jump in, anything you guys wanted to add?
Question, anything like that, or should we just get into it?
Yeah, we're ready, gentlemen.
Right.
As Justin's sharing his screen, the last thing that I'll share… so, Adam, you spoke to it, like, we've become quite popular.
So, just so you're aware, as Justin said, we've transitioned the vast majority of the Radix customers over to our platform.
We do have over 5 million paying units on the platform.
7 of the top 10 operators in the industry selected us to be their market survey provider, 33 of the top 50, so… sounds like you're already aware, your client should be aware of us.
We've never lost an accuracy audit in comparison to any of the providers.
it's the easiest product in the industry to use as well.

So, Justin will walk you through it, hopefully you find the same, and then all of the outputs, all the reporting that you'll, want to send to clients eventually will show you how highly branded and easy to read it is, too.
So, just wanted to share that.
And we'll take it from there.
Thank you.
I'll take you through it, and I think these first two sections will be a little bit of a refresher.
maybe a different view here and there, but the My Properties section, this will be your market surveys.
So the way this is set up is that each one of your properties would get their own individual property card, and then all of the data is based off of that property's specific comp set.
So, like you said, you guys know your markets.
If you just want to provide us with a list of comps, we can set them up that way.
Our system can also make some suggestions or recommendations, but what's great is that your team can always add or remove a property to your comp set directly in the application anytime you'd like.
So always in complete control of whoever you do track and follow.

If you click into here, again, just a reminder that this data is updated every single morning, but what's nice is we do give you the ability to aggregate it over whatever timeframe is relevant to you.
So if you're having a monthly or quarterly review with an investor or with a client, you can pick and choose whatever timeframe you want to look at.
Like Corey mentioned, we have both PDF and Excel exports that you can download on demand.
You can also automate them to be delivered to an inbox on whatever frequency or cadence you'd like.
And then in the application, what's nice is it's… pretty manipulable.
I mean, you can filter this data however you'd like.
So you can look at it based on specific comps, or even subset of comps.
You can look at it based off of bedroom or bathroom combos, square footage ranges, rent price ranges.
We even have the ability to filter in or out renovated units, classic units, penthouses, affordable units.
Are you guys in the BTR space at all?
Yeah.
We manage the most, here in Phoenix, yes.
So I'll let you know, we have the largest definitive BTR database in the entire industry.
So…
Okay?
Awesome for that, keep that in mind.

Also, down below here on the left, all these metrics are going to be sortable from low to high, or high to low, so if you want to see where you stack up, you know, based off of occupancy, or leasing velocity, or which property has… is closest to yours, you can sort… you can filter that, or sort that however you'd like.
And then on the right, just to show you this, as far as editing comps go, all you need to do is click Edit Comps.
From here on the right, we'll give you a map view of the area.

So the blue pin represents your property, the white pins would be properties that have already been added to your comp set, and the black pins are properties that are in the area that you might want to consider adding as well.
So all you gotta do is click Add Comp, all of the data plus the historicals are instantly added to your comp set.
Major callout over here on the left with this list view is that we do assign a comp score to every property in the vicinity of yours.
And this is going to be a correlation score, or essentially a reference point in terms of how strong our system thinks a comp is.
And these scores here, they're derived by 5 factors.
So we look at things like distance or proximity to your property, we also look at vintage, total units, and then we do some pricing and amenity matching.
So those 5 factors spit out that score, so it'll be objectively or very directionally correct, but at the end of the day, it's your call.
You can track as few or as many properties as you want for each comp set.
Let me ask you this, so when you… I know you have a different platform for the Red.
When you're going into at the comps, and you're in the, You're in the satellite view, or the map view.
Are BTR SFR townhome products still in?
that view?
So whenever you… yeah, good question.
So whenever you click into here, we're gonna show you everything.
So BTO… I mean, it'll be a 4…
Okay.
senior everything.
What's nice, though, is at the top, we have all these filtering options.
So if you want to look at certain vintage, or you want to include or exclude BTRs, or property class, or even amenity mixes, you can.
So, I would say, like, in a dense market, I mean, like, this is Nashville, this is pretty extreme.
You know, use these filters to really hone in on what you care about, but yeah, it'll initially show everything.
And when it comes to property class, how do you guys define… because it can be subjective based on, you know, your opinion in some cases.
Do you do it just by vintage?
Now, there's 3 factors.
So, property class, it's gonna be based off of relative quality, location, and condition.
So, those 3 factors…
book.
Or, er, in on it.
So if you have… I mean, do you have a cutoff on the vintage?
Like, if you're not gonna be pulling a 1995 product into Class A, are you?
Well, if it's renovated, we would be.
If not, it wouldn't be.
If it was built 95 renovated in 22, you know, it could be, obviously, but not if it's not renovated.
Yeah.
I can get your money.
specifics on that, actually.
No, no, it's great.
I just… I'm just coming off the top of my head as you run through it.
Yeah, spit them out if you got them.
But yeah, I'll give you the high level of everything here.
I'm not gonna drag it through the weeds, but, like, the market survey, it's broken into five sections.
So the first one's property summary.
This is, like, your high-level property data.
So how far away each comp is, how many units, who manages them.
We give you links to the Google reviews.
Down below here, we'll let you know if there is a rent special working session tied to the property, or even specific floor plans or units in the property.
We can present concessions, then, as either a percentage of rent or as a raw value.
And then we will give you both gross and net rents as well.
So, that's kind of part one of the market survey.
Part two is where you're going to get those performance metrics.
So, your occupancy, lease exposure, percentages.
We'll hit on availability and then show things like applications, cancels, denials, and leases.

And I always say, what's nice about, kind of, the public data model is that if you guys have a question at all about where a metric, a calculation, a concession, where anything's coming from, we can trace it all back to the source.
So it's not like I picked up the phone and talked to a leasing agent that said, hey, we're at 98%, you jump on their website and they're offering a month free.
So very easy to kind of track the information.
What else I'd like is you can drill into all these data points.
So if you want to see the 25 units that are available at 1200 Broadway, you can click into there and see those 25 specific units.
You want to see the 7 units that have leased over the last week, you can drill into there and see those 7 units.
So, very easy to audit the data as well, and kind of follow the lifetime journey of a unit, if you'd like.
So, so how do you get that?
You just… you, you're, like, web scraping and pretty much seeing, like, when it goes offline, you consider it leased?
Is that… If I remember correctly.
Yeah, essentially.
So, if a unit's available on the website today, it falls under the available unit bucket.
If tomorrow that unit's gone from the website, we're going to assume that somebody put in an application on it.
And then if that unit doesn't reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled or denied, then it becomes a lease.
So, leases over the last 7 days is technically, like, leases over the last 8 to 14 days.
We just have that 7-day grace period for a cancel or a denial.
Cool.
Yeah, so I mean, internally, if you.
I actually…
Sorry, good.

I actually have a question, and we might get to it later, for properties that are… Coming onto market, so newly developed properties that are maybe delivering units over a few months, as opposed to all at once.

How would you track… The occupancy levels for a property that is delivering, let's say, 25 units a month, but it's 250 units total, because oftentimes they're advertised You know, availability on their website is… You know, hard to tell how many agents they've actually…
